findings.description: The memo header fields (To, From, Subject) are rendered as a bordered table in HTML, whereas in the PDF they are presented as simple labeled text without visible table borders.
findings.evidence: PDF shows To/From/Subject as plain labeled text; HTML renders them inside a table with visible cell borders and shading.

findings.description: The report outline (sections I-IV) is rendered as a bordered table in HTML, whereas in the PDF it is a simple numbered list.
findings.evidence: PDF shows I-IV as an indented list; HTML renders them inside a table with visible borders and row shading.

findings.description: The 'Groundwater Assessments' bullet in the oral comments section is not formatted as a separate top-level bullet in the PDF — it appears as a sub-section under 'Excavation' without its own bullet point. The HTML preserves this same structure, but in both versions it could be confusing.
findings.evidence: In PDF page 3, Groundwater Assessments appears indented under Excavation without a bullet; HTML mirrors this structure.

findings.description: The memo header fields (To, From, Subject) and the report outline (I-IV) are rendered as HTML tables, which is a reasonable structural choice but the original PDF uses simple label-value formatting, not tabular data. No data is lost.
findings.evidence: PDF shows 'To:', 'From:', 'Subject:' as simple memo fields; HTML renders them as <table> elements with rows.

findings.description: The 'Groundwater Assessments' bullet under oral comments (Section II) is missing its bullet marker. In the PDF, this item has a bullet point like the other items. In the HTML, it uses 'list-style-type: none', removing the bullet and making it appear as a continuation rather than a separate list item.
findings.evidence:
PDF page 3: '• Groundwater Assessments – Commenters referenced...' has a bullet. HTML: '
  • Groundwater Assessments...' explicitly removes the bullet.

findings.description: The document has nested <main> elements - one in the page structure and another inside the body content. The heading 'MEMORANDUM' is rendered as h1, and the section headings (I, II, III, IV) as h2, which is a reasonable hierarchy. No heading text is incorrect.
findings.evidence: HTML has <main> wrapping the outer structure and another <main> inside it.

findings.description: No fabricated content detected. All text, names, dates, statistics, and details in the HTML trace back to the source PDF.
findings.evidence: Checked all proper nouns (Andrew Pitner, Steve Lanter, Dr. Steven Campbell, Dr. Richard Spruill, Dr. Vengosh, HDR Inc.), dates (March 22 2016, April 22 2016, April 18 2016, May 18 2016, Dec 31 2015, February 29 2016, February 2014), numbers (190 attendees, 21 registered speakers, 9 additional, 97 letters, 873 emails, $10 billion, 0.7 ug/L, 1.73 ug/L, 17%, 34%, 55%, 70 feet, 150 feet, 14 power plants), and organizations — all match PDF.

findings.description: The introductory paragraph and outline (I-IV) from page 1 appear to be present in both the header area and the body of the HTML. The memo text on page 1 about the meeting purpose, review of comments, and the outline structure is effectively rendered once, but the page footer from page 1 (State of North Carolina | Environmental Quality...) appears once appropriately.
findings.evidence: The content appears only once in the HTML body section. No actual duplication found upon closer review.
